verdict.answer_first: B gives the core answer faster: “REST is an architectural style…as a set of constraints”; A opens with context before “REST is an architectural style…”
verdict.decisiveness: A commits harder to a strict standard: “Drop one and it's not REST,” while B softens with “to the degree it satisfies them.”
verdict.fabrication: B adds unsupported named examples: “GitHub, Stripe, Twitter”; A’s broad “Most ‘REST APIs’ in the wild are Level 2” is also unsupported, but less specific.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it ends with a plain practical checklist: “Resource-oriented URIs… HTTP methods… Meaningful status codes… Statelessness… JSON representations.”
